steps.title: Identify what the blank needs to do
steps.content: The subject "Some historians" is a plural noun phrase that needs a finite main verb to complete the clause. The blank is followed by the object clause "that this 'tulip mania' was..." Only a finite verb can head a main clause — nonfinite forms (participles, infinitives) cannot.

steps.title: Test each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — claiming: present participle (nonfinite). Cannot serve as the main verb of the clause — leaves "Some historians" without a finite verb. ✗
  • Choice B — claim: finite present tense, third-person plural — matches the plural subject "Some historians" and correctly heads the main clause. ✓
  • Choice C — having claimed: perfect participle (nonfinite). Same problem as A — cannot function as a main verb. ✗
  • Choice D — to claim: to-infinitive (nonfinite). Cannot serve as the main verb of the clause — also leaves "Some historians" without a finite verb. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
